As a genetic bio-engineer, I am working on architecting the growth of organs and appendages via biological induction grafting. For you dummies, that means I do things like grow human ears on mice. It's super cool.

One day I fell asleep at work, in the lab, and a couple of the office knuckleheads decided to prank yours truly. Here's what went down. See, we had this dick in the freezer - some Japanese guy kicked off and donated it to us. It's in his will and everything. Pretty wild, right?

So, other than occasionally pulling it out of the fridge and sticking it in Benny's ear for a goof, it doesn't get much action.

Currently we're working on growing a thumb - it's pretty great because all the mice look like they're doing the Fonz's "Heyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy" as they have a big thumb sticking up on their backs.

Because we're so tied up with the thumb, we have no time to mess around with the freezer dick. Eventually we'll learn how to grow dicks, although more people need new thumbs than dicks, you know?

But, back to my story.

I passed out around noon after a particularly heavy Arby's beef and cheddar lunch. It was one of those five-for-five deals, and I put down the sandwiches in about seven minutes.

When I woke up, Carl was pointing at my hand and laughing. Those bozos has grafted the Japanese dick to

Edit 9/16/20: This is by far one of my sons favorite trash truck toys. We have had it for a few weeks now and he gravitates towards this little truck, every single day.

The dumping action of the trash can is awesome and fluid. My son can easily do it Everytime and the way it is shaped on top, all of the buttons and extra stuff we use go in everytime, he absolutely loves it.

The wheels spin well and freely and my son will bend over and push it around the floor through every room, it moves and keeps up with him. The little dumping action arm folds up and sits really well on the side of the truck so he can push it around but opens easily so he can dump his trash can in.

The toughest part for my son is getting the trash can out of the clip (or back in it), it has a really strong/tight grip on the trash can. It's awesome because the trash can stays put while playing but if he wants to take the trash can off, it can be tough but, he's almost 2 so he is getting better with it.

Ultimately, still an awesome trash truck that is getting played with daily!

top_profane.text:
If you've playing Dominion, you may have noticed that each set seems to be getting more advanced (challenging), and Dark Ages is the most advanced set yet. Don't play it with those brand new to the game as many of the card mechanics may be too much to grasp for novices. Save it til they get used to the other expansions and then watch their eyes light up. For experienced players, however, right out of the box, you know you've got something special here. It's a game-changing set focusing on a grim-n-gritty Dark Ages theme that concentrates a lot of its efforts on trashing cards for fun and profit.

Shelters replace starting estates (one is useful as it gives +2 actions, the other two either provide +1 card when you trash them or a way to trash them when you buy a Victory card). You can't get more Shelters than the three you begin with and Estates are still available to buy.

Ruins are a new action card supply pile that provide crappy bonuses. These get dished out like Curses through attack cards and are meant to clog up opponents' hands. Ex: +1 card, or +1 buy, or look at the top 2 cards of your draw pile and keep or discard them). One card, Death Cart, self-inlicts two ruins on you when you buy it, but it acts as a Platinum (+5 money) and only costs 4 money to get. But you must trash an action card from your hand when you play it or else you trash the Death
top_profane.title: An advanced expansion set for experienced players

I bought these for my son, who loves garbage trucks. I also bought dollhouse food and drink for him to use as some three-dimensional trash. (The name of the listing on Amazon starts off "100 Pieces Miniature Food Drink Bottles" if you want to look for the same set.) The 100 pieces of trash fill the six trash cans, as you can see in the picture. I haven't given the trash cans to my son yet, so I can't comment on their durability yet.

The English is not great on the trash cans or the trash cards. (Examples: A toilet is labeled as "bad closestool." Shoes are spelled "shose." A band-aid is labeled "woundplast."). How you are supposed to classify some of the waste is also not clear. I mean, how on earth am I supposed to tell my child the difference between "non-recyclable garbage," "throw the rubbish at hand," and "other waste?" But, really, I know he will have fun just using the cards as trash, as he has a Green Toys recycling truck that has really small holes for trash, and those cards should fit perfectly. The front of the cards is plastic-y, but the back side is paper (so it's kind of like they were laminated on one side).

As I mentioned, my son hasn't received these yet, but I know they will be a big hit because he has a garbage truck at his grandparents that came with a trash and recycle can, and he loves playing with those.
top_profane.title: Fun Toy Trash Cans

[[VIDEOID:8b8aeb6353fb1da80396fcde349359ea]] Cool car but has issues. It’s a pain to put together and the instructions aren’t great.

At initial try, turning radius sucks as it wouldn’t turn left very well. After doing some mechanics, I discovered the problem - turning motor and gears are crap. Don’t expect it to turn well on the remote control. Take the steering motor out and it turns MUUUUCH nicer.

UPDATE:

It a year later and I love this car (but not because I suddenly learned I was doing something wrong and now it’s awesome). Rather, as it stands, it’s a piece of shit. Battery is crap, motors are slow and turning radius is crap because of the turning motor (and just poor design). However, after getting bug for mods, here is what I had to do.

-upgraded motors from MLToys. Still 12v, but MUCH better.
-Put in 2 12v batteries in parallel so the car runs longer.
-Removed the steering motor - turning is easier and better radius - remote steering SUCKS!!!!
-Upgraded control box to one that can do 24v. Eventually I want to make this car faster and let my kid FLY!!!
-Completely cleaned and re-lubed gear boxes - grease used is sticky and gear boxes don’t move well making car slow.

Summary: If you already bought one, take some time to do some fixing up and it will be a LOT better. Uenjoy makes a cool looking car
top_profane.title: JUST GET A POWER WHEELS AND SAVE YOURSELF THE HEADACHE!!!

The only way you can dump out the trash from the back is to lift the entire truck into the air and turn it 45 degrees so it is upside down and the trash falls out.

Originally posted at Throat Punch Games, a new idea every day!

Product- Shadowrun 6th Ed.
System-Shadowrun 6th Ed.
Producer- Catalyst Game Lab
Price- $39.99
TL; DR- New but similar. 93%

Basics- Shadowrun is 30 years old! In honor of this, here is the 6th edition. What changed? What's new? What's old? Let's dive in together!

Basics- Shadowrun 6th ed. uses the solid base of 5th edition. Almost all rolls are: take an attribute (the stats of your character that range mostly between 1 to 6), add your skill ranks, and roll that many dice. You then count the number of 1s and the 5s and 6s. 5 and 6 are successes and used to determine if you succeed, while if you have over half 1s, that's a glitch and something bad happens. Pretty simple.

What's new with the basics-the BIGGEST change between editions is edge. Edge is the magic cheaty story currency. You buy pizza? Get a point of edge. You roleplay well? Edge. You're the only player paying attention and you catch the plot hook? EDGE! What changed is now before every roll we check who has an advantage in a situation. That side gains edge now. You see in the dark and the opponent doesn't? Edge for you! What's also changed is edge isn't just rerolls now. It's much more an a la carte menu where you choose abilities, to change dice faces, and other crazy op

Clank! In! Space!
2-4 players, Competitive

Overview of Gameplay-

For those wondering right off the bat how much different this is from the original Clank! I can tell you that it is very similar with some added (awesome) tweaks. Overall the biggest difference is going to be the space theme when compared to original Clanks! Fantasy dungeon theme. I’ll go more into those differences later on.

The way this game plays is in my opinion a masterpiece of game design. Disclaimer, I love the original Clank and low and behold I love Clank! In! Space! as well. Up to 4 players sneak aboard Lord Eradikus’s ship and plot to steal some of his precious artifacts and try to make it back to the escape pods to freedom. To do this you will draw a hand of 5 cards from your personal deck of cards, which, all players start with the exact same 10 cards in their deck at the very beginning so everyone is basically on equal footing. Then on your turn you play all of the 5 cards you drew and take the actions listed on them. The three main actions are Move, buy more cards and attack monsters. Any cards you buy from a row of 6 cards go directly into your discard pile and these all get shuffled back into a draw deck once your draw pile depletes. So over time your deck of cards gets stronger and you can effectively mold your deck the way you want based on what cards you ch

top_rant.text:
A neuronphaser.com review.

CONTENT(5/5)

The Dungeon Masters Guide is always a tough book to review, and I bet that’s because it’s an even tougher book to write. You’ve got new folks coming to D&D for the first time, sophomores coming from just one other edition of D&D to this new one (and “edition” in D&D terms is like the differences between an apple and psychometry: just totally different!), and you’ve got the grognards and edition warriors coming out of the mists of legend to check out the new beast on the block. It has to serve so many masters, cover so many topics, and somehow channel the best of rules mechanics, guidelines, functional tips, and enforcing social contracts. You almost can’t win.

The folks who wrote 5th Edition's DMG pulled out all the stops, and they succeeded.

Part 1: The Top-Down Approach

Part 1 of the DMG tackles the big-picture stuff, which is worth your perusal even if you run published campaign settings, as it covers a lot of ground from the nitty-gritty (Factions, languages, how settlements work) to the more theoretical (play style, overarching campaign-shattering events).

The first chapter is an exercise in taking the big-picture stuff about creating a campaign setting that looks and feels like a Dungeons & Dragons world, figuring out a playstyle that inspires both DM and Players with
top_rant.title: One of the best, most immediately useful D&D reference books for at-the-table use ever written!

My 5-year-old has been watching Octonauts for a few years, and it's one of the few kids' shows I actually like. As a rule, I hate most modern kids' programming, especially Nick crap, but even a lot of the PBS shows. Most kids' shows are garbage--ugly, badly written, too focused on characters who are jerks, of just 20-minute commercials for whatever crappy toy. Octonauts, on the other hand, is great. Yes, it ignores most of the food chain, but introduces kids to a myriad of fascinating creatures, promotes conservation and teamwork, poo-poos silly superstitions, and has female characters in smart (if supporting, my only quibble) roles. And it's adorable. Along with Ocean Mysteries & Zoobomafoo, it's really cultivated an enthisiasm for animals and wild nature in my daughter. So, unlike stupid Dora and Disney princesses and the rest, I'm totally happy for her to play with these licensed characters.

I've bought her a few pieces here and there, but it's hard to track down anyone but the 3 male leads in the U.S. This set has the whole octopod, plus a gup, and the whole core gang! It's fun and expansive, and works great with a collection of animal toys, especially marine animals. This map is incredible. And the case that comes with it is durable and a great value.
It’s worth it to buy it just for those things. I’ve used it for several weeks now and I love it.
However, be aware that you need to buy some markers to go with it. The markers it comes with are pretty much garbage. You can get a nice set of washable Crayola markers at a JoAnns or Michaels for cheap if you like a marker with a wide tip. Or wet erase Expo markers on Amazon if you like a fine tip (my preference). Just remember, DRY ERASE BAD. I also recommend throwing the erasss it comes with in the trash and investing in some dollar store wash cloths and putting a couple cloths in the case for easy cleanup, it’s best to wipe it down with one damp cloth and then wipe it dry with a fresh cloth. The erasers it comes with are very bad and a cloth will clean it perfectly.
The clips it comes with are incredibly flimsy. One of them came broken. So don’t expect a lot out of the crap metal clips it comes with.
Do not let the crummy clips, erasers, or markers dissuade you, this map is incredibly high-quality. Definitely worth the investment. And when considering the cost of buying a separate case for a map, this makes it an extremely valuable purchase for the money and quality. Listen, if you are buying a map, BUY A CASE. I cannot stress that enough. Maps are a money investment. And

top_five_star_obscene.text: Our son loves this dump truck. We love how it does not require any batteries and has no noises (we have so many toys that use batteries and have noises). The build quality of this toy is durable and has not broken yet even though our son sometimes plays a little rough with it. Our son hasn't gotten the hang of pushing the top button multiple types (hydraulics) to make the dump truck go up, but he's getting there. The only downside with this toy is that it comes with a blue garbage can that attaches to the back and it falls off of the hinges pretty easily. I don't think it is a big deal though. Our son doesn't even really play with the garbage can. He just enjoys throwing things into the garbage truck. The inside of the garbage truck is holly and can hold a lot of things. Kids will love throwing random things into the garbage truck. Good size truck for toddlers.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Good simple garbage truck that does not need batteries!

top_five_star_obscene.text: This is my first non-powered blaster and I must say I'm impressed.  The blaster is small and light weight, but fits my adult hand very well.  It is easy to cock and load and gets decent range for such a simple design (25 to 30 feet with the included whistler darts, also with regular streamline darts, only 15 to 20 feet with suction cup darts, and a nice 35 to 40 feet with the elite darts).  It accecpts a variety of dart styles (I have used WHistlers, Suction cup, Regular Streamlines, and Elites).  There is a nice Top tactical rail and towords the back of the rail is a small button.  Depressing this fires the baster (a very cool feature if your being taken prisoner and have to turn over your blaster, you can get one more shot off without touching the trigger!).  Loading is a simple as can be (cock the gun and push a dart into the barrel).  Ialso love the fact that it holds 2 extra darts below the barrel (a real plus if your running low on darts in the heated part of the battle).  I got my 2 pack on clearence at a local store for ten dollars off what it is here.  Not sure if the price here is a little high as I've only found these at one local store and like I say they were on clearence.  About the only thing I don't like about these blasters is that the slide tends to be a little loose so it rattles somewhat (as most slide blasters do).  All in all these are a cool addition to s
top_five_star_obscene.title: Compact sidearm

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I got this as a fun way to spend my time on the beach. This was cheap enough and had an included camera so I went for it - not expecting much. Holy crap is this thing fun. One of the best investments I made. It flies really well - and has some cool features like doing barrel rolls and having options for low and high speed.

This thing was surviging on the beach with relatively high winds - i was impressed. Of course I lost control of it several times - but it held up through the crashes and sand.

Taking images and videos is a pain on this thing - it is hard to tell when its on or not. Also you have to turn off the camera first before turningn the drone off or else the data will become corrupted. Further, the image and video quality is somewhat shitty. The video - when the drone is stabilized - is OK but there is no gyro or stabilizer for the camera so do not expect much.

If you are looking to fly a drone for the first time this one is great - if you want to dish out extra dough look for drones that have a larger range (100m).
top_five_star_obscene.title: Great beginner drone!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
My first purchase of the Nerf N-Strike Jolt Blaster was strictly based on the small size since the recipient was my 3-year old grandson. I have to admit that I was shocked at the speed of the darts, and not just "it's powerful for its size". It's powerful compared to any Nerf gun regardless of size and cost. So, I bought it again.

I will say that a 3-year old is not strong enough to cock this, so I have to continually cock it for him. No big deal.

I wanted another Jolt before they went up in cost, and at $5.99 I am satisfied that I got a nice deal. The only other gun I considered was the Triad, which is essentially the same size frame with a bit more bulk because you can load 3 darts on it.

You won't be disappointed with the Jolt.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Had to get another Jolt
